      "question": "A metric space (X, d) is said to be arc-wise connected if for any two points x, y in X, there exists a continuous function f: [0, 1] -> X such that f(0) = x and f(1) = y. Which of the following is a correct statement about arc-wise connectedness?",
      "options": [
        "A) X is locally connected",
        "B) X is connected",
        "C) Every continuous function f: [0, 1] -> X is linear",
        "D) There exists a connected subset U of X such t
